CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-13-2022 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. Notice having been given and there being no appearance or objection filed, the Court orders the case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. JUDGE: ANNE-CHRISTINE MASSULLO; Clerk: GGonzales; Proceedings not reported.

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-07-2022 Continued To Sep-13-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-20-2022 Continued To Jun-14-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-08-2021 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Feb-08-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-08-2021 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-05-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES (via Zoom):

Hunter W. Sims III; Deputy City Attorney; 415-554-4259; appearing on behalf of defendant City and County of San Francisco.
Howard Olsen; Law Offices of Michael Heath; 415-931-4207; appearing on behalf of plaintiff Brennan 969 Market Street LLC.

Plaintiff contests the court's tentative ruling. The court and the parties discuss the case City of Oroville vs. Superior Court of Butte County (2019) 7 Cal.5th 1091. The court asks the opposing party if they can point out any evicence that shows, using the Oroville test, that the damage in question was substantially caused by inherent risks associated with design , construction, or maintenence. Mr. Olsen argues that the evidence submitted regarding expert witness Warren Best establishes a triable issue of fact as to whether flooding was a result of the City's inherent design flaw. Mr. Sims argues that the evidence submitted does not show an inherent risk assocated with the design. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. There is a triable issue of material of fact as to whether or not it was a design flaw that is inherently risky or whether it is the backflow.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
